title: Heteropterys hiraeoides
intro: Heteropterys hiraeoides is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae, commonly known as a type of mulberry tree or a member of the velvet tree family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Heteropterys hiraeoides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Heteropterys hiraeoides is often prepared as a tea or infused in water to create a decoction, which is used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Heteropterys hiraeoides is a plant species that belongs to the family Malpighiaceae.
